display_name: FirstProof P6: ε-light 子集存在性
benchmark_scope: Prove there exists an absolute constant c > 0 such that for every graph G and every epsilon in (0,1), V contains an epsilon-light subset S of size at least c * epsilon * |V|.
planning_constraints: Start with computational experiments on small graphs (K_n, cycles, paths, random graphs) to build intuition for what epsilon-light sets look like.
The probabilistic method is likely the right approach: random sampling with appropriate probability.
Consider both independent vertex sampling and more structured approaches (e.g., greedy, low-degree vertices first).
Matrix concentration inequalities may be needed to control the PSD condition.
Do not assume the result; derive every step from the seeds.
